You are verifier #1, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

Finding F-029: "No SLO-relevant metrics tracked: run duration, corpus size, and claim count absent"
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (line 515)
Description: Tracker logs alignment_rate and verdict counts but never logs: run wall-clock duration, corpus size (n_units), claim count (n_claims), baseline build time. Cannot detect slow baselines or runaway calls.
PoC: Run 10 studies of increasing corpus size. Query MLflow store — no n_units, n_claims, or run_duration_s metrics.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
2. Look at the code at line 515 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

481	                run_gate(
482	                    GateReview(
483	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
484	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
485	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
486	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
487	                        payload={
488	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
489	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),

494	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
495	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
496	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
497	                for h in hypotheses:
498	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
499	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
500	                        raise FirewallViolation(
501	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
502	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
503	                        )
504	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
505	                    trail.record(
506	                        "verdict",
507	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
508	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
509	                        label=verdict.label.value,
510	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
511	                    )
512	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
513	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
514	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
515	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
516	
517	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
518	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
519	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
520	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
521	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
522	            if not claim_records:
523	                raise ValueError(
524	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
525	                )
526	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
527	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
528	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
529	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
530	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
531	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
532	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
533	            run_gate(
534	                GateReview(
535	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
536	                    frm=prev_phase,
537	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
538	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
539	                    payload={
540	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
541	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
542	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
543	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
544	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
545	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
546	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
547	                    },
548	                )
549	            )
550	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
551	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
552	
553	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
554	                    trail.record(
555	                        "preregister",
556	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
557	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
558	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
559	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,

560	                    )
561	                    trail.record(
562	                        "verdict",
563	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
564	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
565	                        label=v.label.value,
566	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
567	                    )
568	
569	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
570	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
571	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
572	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
573	                    baseline,
574	                    claim_records,
575	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
576	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
577	                    authority=plan.authority,
578	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
579	                    source_name=defn.name,
580	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
581	                )
582	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
583	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
584	                trail.record(
585	                    "score",
586	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
587	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
588	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
589	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
590	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
591	                )
592	                sc = scorecard
593	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
594	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
595	                track(
596	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
597	                )
598	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
599	                if ar is not None:
600	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
601	
602	        # ---- REPORT ----
603	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
604	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
605	            trail.record(
606	                "report",
607	                "assembled reproducibility package",
608	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
609	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
610	            )
611	
612	        if tuple(visited) != required:
613	            raise FirewallViolation(
614	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
615	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
616	            )
617	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
618	        ok = True
619	    except Exception as exc:
620	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
621	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
622	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
623	        try:
624	            trail.record(
625	                "run_failed",
626	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
627	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
628	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
629	                last_phase=last_phase,
630	            )
631	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
632	            pass
633	        raise
634	    finally:
635	        trace_ctx.close()
636	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
637	            try:
638	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
639	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
640	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
641	                pass
642	
643	    return StudyResult(
644	        study=defn.name,
645	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
646	        baseline=baseline,
647	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
648	        scorecard=scorecard,
649	        phases=tuple(visited),
650	        provenance=trail.entries,
651	        source_version=source_version,
652	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
653	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
654	    )

content.content:
1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import uuid
33	from contextlib import ExitStack
34	from dataclasses import dataclass
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, cast
37	
38	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value
39	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
40	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
41	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
42	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
43	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
44	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
45	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
46	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
47	    ClaimConfirmer,
48	    SourceScorecard,
49	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
50	)
51	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
52	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
53	    ClaimBlindGuard,
54	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
55	    FirewallViolation,
56	)
57	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
58	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
59	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
60	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
61	    OtelTracer,
62	    Tracer,
63	    bootstrap_tracing,
64	    run_trace_context,
65	)
66	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
67	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
68	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
69	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
70	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
71	
72	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
73	
74	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
75	
76	
77	class IngestionError(ValueError):
78	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
79	
80	
81	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
82	class GateReview:
83	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
84	
85	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
86	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
87	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
88	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
89	    """
90	
91	    gate: str
92	    frm: Phase
93	    to: Phase
94	    summary: str
95	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
99	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
100

200	            ]
201	            if missing:
202	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
203	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
204	            missing = [
205	                n
206	                for n, v in (
207	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
208	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
209	                )
210	                if v is None
211	            ]
212	            if missing:
213	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
214	
215	
216	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
217	class StudyResult:
218	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
219	
220	    study: str
221	    corpus_fingerprint: str
222	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
223	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
224	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
225	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
226	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
227	    source_version: str | None = None
228	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
229	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
230	
231	
232	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
233	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
234	
235	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
236	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
237	
238	    return _log
239	
240	
241	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
242	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
243	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
244	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
245	
246	
247	def run_study(
248	    plan: StudyPlan,
249	    *,
250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
253	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
254	    clock: Clock | None = None,
255	    secret: bytes | None = None,
256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
275	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
276	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
277	    defn = plan.definition
278	    modes = defn.modes
279	    required = required_phases(modes)
280	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
281	    visited: list[Phase] = []
282	    run_id: str | None = None
283	
284	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
285	        visited.append(phase)
286	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
287	
288	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
289	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
290	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
291	            return
292	        try:
293	            fn(tracker, run_id)
294	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
295	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
296	
297	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
298	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
299	        decision = gate_handler(review)
300	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
301	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
302	        trail.record(
303	            "gate",
304	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
305	            gate=review.gate,
306	            approved=approved,
307	            reason=reason,
308	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
309	        )
310	        if not approved:
311	            raise GateError(
312	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
313	            )
314	
315	    trail.record(
316	        "run_start",
317	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
318	        study=defn.name,
319	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),

320	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
321	    )
322	    if tracker is not None:
323	        try:
324	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
325	                defn.name,
326	                {
327	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
328	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
329	                },
330	            )
331	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
332	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
333	
334	    ok = False
335	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
336	    try:
337	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
338	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
339	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
340	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
341	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
342	        bootstrap_tracing()
343	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
344	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
345	        # ---- INGEST ----
346	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
347	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
348	            try:
349	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
350	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
351	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
352	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
353	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
354	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
355	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
356	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
357	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
358	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
359	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
360	                raise IngestionError(
361	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
362	                )
363	            if not corpus.units:
364	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
365	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
366	            source_version: str | None = None
367	            if versioner is not None:
368	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
369	            trail.record(
370	                "ingest",
371	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
372	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
373	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
374	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
375	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
376	                source_version=source_version,
377	            )
378	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
379	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
380	                assert plan.split is not None
381	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
382	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
383	                if unknown:
384	                    raise FirewallViolation(
385	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
386	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
387	                    )
388	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
389	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
390	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
391	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
392	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
393	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
394	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
395	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
396	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
397	                    raise FirewallViolation(
398	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
399	                    )
400	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
401	                if ghost:
402	                    raise FirewallViolation(
403	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
404	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
405	                    )
406	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
407	                trail.record(
408	                    "features",
409	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
410	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
411	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
412	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
413	                )
414	
415	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
416	        baseline_instant = _now()
417	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
418	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
419	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
420	            with guard.sealed():
421	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
422	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
423	                raise FirewallViolation(
424	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
425	                    "was not built from this corpus"
426	                )
427	            trail.record(
428	                "baseline",
429	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
430	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
431	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
432	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
433	            )
434	
435	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
436	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
437	
438	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
439	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
440	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
441	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
442	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
443	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
444	
445	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
446	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
447	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
448	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
449	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
450	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
451	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
452	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
453	                trail.record(
454	                    "discovery",
455	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
456	                    n=len(hypotheses),
457	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
458	                )
459	
460	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
461	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
462	                if not held_out.units:
463	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
464	                preregister_instant = _now()
465	                for h in hypotheses:
466	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
467	                        raise FirewallViolation(
468	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
469	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
470	                        )
471	                    vt = require_preregistered(
472	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
473	                    )
474	                    trail.record(
475	                        "preregister",
476	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
477	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
478	                        digest=vt.digest,
479	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),

content.text: Now I have a comprehensive view of the pipeline. Let me search specifically for any tracking of `n_units`, `n_claims`, or `run_duration` in the tracker (not just trail):

input.description: Search for metric logging and timing in pipeline.py

content.content:
236:        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
242:    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
374:                n_units=len(corpus.units),
411:                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
540:                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
593:                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
594:                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))

content.text:
This is conclusive. The `n_units` and `n_claims` values appear only in `trail.record()` calls (provenance audit trail), NOT in `track()` calls to the experiment tracker. The only metrics sent to the experiment tracker via `track()` are:

- `discovery_supported`, `discovery_contradicted`, `discovery_indeterminate` (verdict counts at line 515)
- `n_supported`, `n_contradicted`, `n_indeterminate`, `alignment_rate` (scorecard metrics at lines 593-600)

There is no `track()` call for `n_units`, `n_claims`, or any run wall-clock duration. The `run_start` timestamp is recorded in the trail but not in the tracker, and no `run_duration_s` is ever computed or logged to the tracker.

input.reasoning: The code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py confirms the finding. The only metrics sent to the experiment tracker via track() are the six verdict/scorecard values: discovery_supported, discovery_contradicted, discovery_indeterminate (line 515), n_supported, n_contradicted, n_indeterminate, and alignment_rate (lines 593-600). While n_units (line 374) and n_claims (line 540) do appear in trail.record() provenance entries, they are never passed to track(), so they never reach the MLflow store. There is no perf_counter or datetime capture around the run, and no run_duration_s metric is ever computed or logged. This means an MLflow query will return no n_units, n_claims, or run_duration_s columns, matching the PoC exactly. The gap is genuine: without corpus size and duration in the tracker, SLO monitoring for slow baselines or runaway call counts requires scraping the provenance trail rather than querying MLflow directly.
